message:
fix(agent-drivers): Eliminate ID generation modulo bias and evict unhealthy drivers

This commit addresses two issues in the agent-drivers package: a subtle
statistical bias in ID generation and stale driver instances remaining
in cache after health check failures.

ID Generation Modulo Bias Fix (base-driver.ts):
- The previous implementation used `byte % charsetLength` which creates
  non-uniform distribution when 256 is not evenly divisible by the
  charset length
- Example: For a 62-char charset, bytes 0-61 map to chars 0-61, but
  bytes 62-255 map to chars 0-7 again, making those 8 characters ~1.6%
  more likely than the other 54
- New implementation uses rejection sampling:
  - Calculate maxValid = 256 - (256 % charsetLength) to find the largest
    multiple of charsetLength that fits in a byte
  - Reject any byte >= maxValid and sample again
  - This ensures perfectly uniform distribution across all charset chars
- Allocate 2x buffer to minimize refills (rare for short IDs)
- This is a security-relevant fix for generating unpredictable IDs

Unhealthy Driver Eviction (registry.ts):
- When getBestDriver() detects an unhealthy driver during selection,
  it now evicts that driver from the instance cache
- This forces recreation on the next request, which may resolve
  transient initialization issues
- Previously, unhealthy drivers would remain cached and repeatedly
  fail health checks until server restart
- The eviction happens before adding the warning, ensuring clean state

These changes improve the reliability and security of agent driver
management without changing the public API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
